title: Coelogyne rhodobulba
intro: Coelogyne rhodobulba, commonly known as the Red-bulb Coelogyne or Red-bulb Orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its potent anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which can help to reduce pain and alleviate symptoms of infections. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne rhodobulba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coelogyne rhodobulba is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and fever.
